dict.string:
KV4P/ATL - Component Legal Index
Developed by Blake Ross WX4ATL
This index is bundled with KV4P/ATL and names the license, copyright, and attribution status of materials shipped with or retained inside the app package. Full license text files are bundled in the same Settings legal area.
1. KV4P/ATL app, BLE firmware overlay, web flasher glue, tools, and icon adaptations License: GNU General Public License, version 3 or later. Full text: GPL-3.0-or-later.txt Copyright: - KV4P HT upstream Android app, firmware, protocol, and artwork: Copyright (C) Vance Vagell and KV4P HT contributors. - KV4P/ATL app, BLE firmware overlay, web flasher glue, tools, and adaptations: Copyright (C) 2026 Blake Ross WX4ATL. Attribution: Developed by Blake Ross WX4ATL.
2. KV4P HT upstream project Source: https://github.com/VanceVagell/kv4p-ht License: GNU General Public License, version 3 or later. Full text: GPL-3.0-or-later.txt Use: firmware base, Android app behavior/protocol reference, and app icon artwork source. Public binary distribution obligations: Preserve copyright/license notices, preserve no-warranty terms, mark modifications, and provide complete corresponding source for the app, firmware overlay, web flasher, build scripts, and assets used to produce the binary release.
3. KV4P HT upstream third-party materials retained from other-licenses.txt Full retained text: KV4PUpstreamOtherLicenses.txt Components covered in that retained upstream notice: - usb-serial-for-android library: MIT License. Copyright (c) 2011-2013 Google Inc. Copyright (c) 2013 Mike Wakerly. - Jost Font: SIL Open Font License, Version 1.1. Copyright 2020 The Jost Project Authors. - arduino-dra818: GNU Lesser General Public License, Version 3. - javAX25: GNU Lesser General Public License, Version 3. - javAPRSlib: GNU Lesser General Public License, Version 3. - BearConsole2: GNU Lesser General Public License, Version 3. - esptool ESP32 stub flasher binaries: Dual Apache License 2.0 or MIT License. - Arduino-audio-tools: GNU General Public License, Version 3. - slf4android: MIT License. - esp32-flash: GNU General Public License, Version 3. - esp32-stubs: MIT License. KV4P/ATL does not link or bundle these Android/web/font helper components directly. The upstream notice text is retained verbatim because this project is a KV4P HT derivative and recipients should be able to inspect the same upstream legal notices.
4. KV4P/ATL app icon and web flasher glyph Source artwork: https://github.com/VanceVagell/kv4p-ht/tree/main/artwork License: GNU General Public License, version 3 or later. Full text: GPL-3.0-or-later.txt Use: Apple Icon Composer app icon and web flasher glyph derived from KV4P radio glyph artwork.
5. ESP Web Tools Source: https://github.com/esphome/esp-web-tools Bundled version: 10.0.1 in web-flasher/esp-web-tools/dist/. License: Apache License 2.0. Full text: Apache-2.0-ESP-Web-Tools.txt Use: Chromium/Web Serial ESP32 firmware flasher. It is bundled with the web flasher package, not linked into the KV4P/ATL app binary.
6. Google Lit and Material Web code inside ESP Web Tools Sources: - https://github.com/lit/lit - https://github.com/material-components/material-web Licenses observed in bundled SPDX comments: - BSD-3-Clause. - Apache-2.0. Full texts: - BSD-3-Clause-Google-Web-Components.txt - Apache-2.0-ESP-Web-Tools.txt Use: transitive bundled web-component/runtime code inside the ESP Web Tools web flasher build.
7. raff/kv4p-go Source: https://github.com/raff/kv4p-go License: MIT License. Full text: MIT-raff-kv4p-go.txt Use: protocol and interoperability reference only; not linked into the KV4P/ATL app binary.
8. kv4p-sharp Source: https://github.com/lupettohf/kv4p-sharp License: GNU General Public License, version 3. Full text: GPL-3.0-or-later.txt Use: protocol and interoperability reference only; not linked into the KV4P/ATL app binary.
9. alta/swift-opus Source: https://github.com/alta/swift-opus License: BSD 3-Clause License. Full text: BSD-3-Clause-alta-swift-opus.txt Use: historical audio reference retained in the corpus. The current app voice path uses local IMA ADPCM code and does not link Opus.
10. APRS and AX.25 protocol specifications Sources: - APRS Working Group, APRS Protocol Reference, Version 1.0.1. - Tucson Amateur Packet Radio Corporation, AX.25 Link Access Protocol for Amateur Packet Radio, Version 2.2. Use: Protocol specification references for locally written packet encoding, decoding, and display logic. These documents are not linked libraries.
11. IMA ADPCM references Sources: - Interactive Multimedia Association ADPCM reference material. - MultimediaWiki IMA ADPCM technical reference. Use: Public algorithm/reference material for the locally written voice codec. No external codec library is linked.
12. APRS map symbols and app UI symbols KV4P/ATL currently displays APRS map markers with SF Symbols and local code-derived APRS symbol metadata. No third-party APRS symbol artwork pack is bundled in the app binary.
13. Apple SDKs and SF Symbols Sources: Apple SDK frameworks used by the app include SwiftUI, AVFAudio, Core Bluetooth, Core Location, MapKit, UIKit, Foundation, and SF Symbols. Use: System SDK functionality supplied by Apple. No Apple framework source or SF Symbols font files are bundled by this app.
14. End User License Agreement and GPL distribution rule Public App Store releases of KV4P/ATL are configured to use the bundled GPL-preserving End User License Agreement text, not Apple's default Standard EULA alone. The agreement states that GPL-3.0-or-later controls for GPL-covered components, does not add further restrictions to GPL rights, and points users to complete corresponding source at https://github.com/WX4ATL/KV4P-ATL.
